oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Tricks and tips in cast/splint application in cattle
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Mulon, Pierre-Yves
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: cattle
fracture
external coaptation
cast
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Fractures are commonly encountered in bovine practice and cattle are great orthopedic patients. While not all fractures can be stabilized with external coaptation, this treatment option is acceptable for a large number of cases with an economical ben­efit. From the field to the barn pen or veterinary facility, emer­gency splinting avoids secondary damage to the fractured leg. An adequate sedation/anesthesia plan is mandatory. Permanent external coaptation is obtained by adequate protection of the bony prominences, inclusion of the foot and immobilization of the joints distal and proximal to the fractured bone.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 114-116
